PERCEPTION: You're seeing things that aren't there. I said what I said.
I don't know what you think you noticed, but that's all there is to it.
Everything looks perfectly normal to me.
I have no idea what you are looking at. There is nothing unusual here.
Stare all you want. You will not find anything I have not already told you.
I do not see what you are pointing at. Maybe you need more sleep.
You keep squinting at things. It is unsettling, frankly.
There is nothing behind the counter. I just swept this morning.
That scratch? It has been there for years. It means nothing.
I think the light is playing tricks on you. Happens a lot in here.
You are looking at the wrong things. Nothing here is worth examining.
That stain? It is from last week's stew. Not everything is a clue.
You are making me nervous with all that looking around. Please stop.
I keep a tidy space. If there were something to notice, I would know.
I assure you, everything here is exactly as it should be.
My eyesight is fine and I see nothing worth remarking on.
You must be tired. Travelers often see things that are not there.
That mark was there when I moved in. It is just old wood.
Whatever you think you spotted, I promise it is ordinary.
The only unusual thing in here right now is you looking under every shelf.
I cleaned this place top to bottom yesterday. There is nothing to find.
You glanced at the window, but there is nothing outside worth noting.
I appreciate the thoroughness, but you are wasting your time.
If there were something out of place, do you not think I would have noticed it myself?
Those are just water stains. This roof leaks when it rains.
I think you are imagining things. It has been a long day for everyone.
I already told you the interesting parts. There is nothing more to see.
You seem to be searching for something that is not here.
My home is unremarkable. I promise you that.
Nothing catches my eye either. I think we are done here.
INSIGHT: You're reading too much into this. It's just a rumor.
I don't appreciate being interrogated. That's all I have to say.
You think you can read people? You are wrong about me.
I am not hiding anything. This is just how I talk.
My face always looks like this. Do not read into it.
You are making assumptions. I am being completely honest with you.
I do not know what expression you think you saw, but I am fine.
I hesitated because I was thinking, not because I was lying.
People always think I am nervous. I just drink too much tea.
That was a cough, not a reaction. I have had this cold for days.
You are not as perceptive as you think you are.
I paused because I forgot what I was saying, nothing more.
My voice sounds the same as it always does.
I look at the door because the draft bothers me. That is all.
I shift around because this chair is uncomfortable, not because I am uneasy.
I do not know why you keep staring at me like that. I have told you everything.
There is no deeper meaning to my tone. I am just tired.
You are searching for something that is not there. I am an open book.
I fidget. I have always fidgeted. Ask anyone.
My wife says I always look guilty, even when I have done nothing.
Whatever you think you are picking up on, you are wrong.
That sigh was because I am bored, not because I am troubled.
I changed the subject because the old one was dull, not suspicious.
You want me to feel guilty about something? I have nothing to feel guilty about.
I yawned because I got up early. Read nothing into it.
Everyone gets a little tense when someone stares at them. That is all this is.
I am being as forthcoming as I know how to be.
Perhaps the problem is that you are looking for something that does not exist.
I rub my neck because it is sore from hauling crates. Do not dramatize it.
I do not appreciate being studied like a specimen. I am just making conversation.
DEDUCTION: You're overthinking this. It is what it is.
Not everything has a hidden explanation. Sometimes a rumor is just a rumor.
I've told you what I know. Picking it apart won't change anything.
That is an interesting theory, but it does not match what actually happened.
You can connect dots all day, but that does not make it true.
I think you are complicating something that is actually quite simple.
The timing is just coincidence. Things happen at the same time sometimes.
I see where you are going with this, and I think you are wrong.
That logic might work in a book, but life is messier than that.
Those two things are not related. You are stretching.
I appreciate the cleverness, but your conclusion does not follow from the facts.
You are looking for a pattern where there is none.
There is a simpler explanation that you seem to be ignoring.
I would not read that much into it. People do strange things for mundane reasons.
Your reasoning has a hole in it, but I cannot be bothered to explain where.
Coincidences happen. Not everything is a conspiracy.
You have built a very elaborate theory on very little evidence.
I see the connection you are trying to make, and it is a reach.
That would make sense if three other things were also true, but they are not.
I think you are letting your imagination fill in the gaps.
The simpler answer is usually the right one. And the simpler answer is boring.
You are assuming things you cannot possibly know for certain.
I follow your reasoning, but I do not agree with your premise.
A clever argument, but no. That is not how it happened.
You are working backward from a conclusion. That is not the same as proof.
I admire the effort, but the facts do not support what you are suggesting.
If it were that obvious, someone would have figured it out by now.
I have heard that theory before. It did not hold up then either.
You are overthinking it. Some things are exactly what they look like.
I wish I could tell you you were right, but I cannot, because you are not.
RECALL: I don't know what archives you've been reading, but that has nothing to do with this.
Old stories are just that: stories. This is different.
Whatever you think you remember, it does not apply here.
I have not heard of anything like that before. Are you sure you are not confused?
That sounds like something from a tale, not something that really happened.
I do not think old histories are relevant to what is going on now.
You know a lot of old names. Shame none of them matter here.
I do not put stock in old records. They get things wrong all the time.
I have never heard of that place. Maybe it does not exist anymore.
That may have been true once, but the world has changed since then.
Old lore is interesting, but it will not explain what happened last week.
I think you have your dates confused. That was a different time entirely.
The past is the past. I am worried about the present.
Whatever old text you are quoting, I guarantee it has been exaggerated over the years.
Names and places from centuries ago do not help with what is happening now.
I have lived here my whole life and I have never heard of such a thing.
That sounds like a scholars' debate, not something a normal person would know.
You are well read, I will give you that. But it is not the same situation.
Maybe in the old days that was true. Not anymore.
I have heard people invoke history before. It never leads anywhere useful.
Those records could easily be wrong. Who checks that sort of thing?
I think you are reaching back further than the facts support.
That symbol could mean anything. Old marks litter half the buildings in town.
I am not a historian. I deal with what is in front of me.
Legends have a way of growing with the telling. I would not rely on them.
Interesting trivia, but I fail to see how it connects.
You are citing events from before anyone alive was born. How is that helpful?
I respect the knowledge, but this is a new problem, not an old one revisited.
Even if that were true once, the landscape has shifted since then.
I do not think reading old books is going to solve this for us.
INTIMIDATION: You don't scare me. I've dealt with worse than you.
Threaten me all you like. I have nothing to say.
Go ahead. See what happens when you push people around in this town.
Looming over me will not change what I know. Which is nothing.
I have had scarier customers during market week.
Are you trying to frighten me? Because it is not working.
People bigger and meaner than you have tried. I am still here.
I do not respond well to threats. You are wasting your breath.
I would be careful with that tone. People around here look out for each other.
That angry look might work on children. I am not one.
You can glare at me all day. My answer will not change.
If you are trying to be threatening, you need more practice.
I grew up with five older siblings. You will have to try harder than that.
Go ahead and raise your voice. It will only attract attention you do not want.
I have been yelled at by professionals. You are an amateur.
The last person who tried to push me around ended up apologizing. Just saying.
I am shaking. Can you tell? No? Because I am not.
I have stared down worse things than you on a bad morning.
That works on the timid. I am not timid.
My mother was scarier than you on her gentlest day. Try again.
If intimidation is your only tool, you are going to run out of options fast.
You can posture all you like. I know nothing worth threatening me over.
I have worked this stall for twenty years. You think I have not been threatened before?
I appreciate the drama, but the answer is still no.
This is a public place. People are watching. Choose your next words carefully.
I am too old and too stubborn to be scared by someone I just met.
That tone does not buy you information. It buys you a closed door.
Anger does not change facts. And the fact is, I cannot help you.
I have nothing to tell you, so there is nothing to force out of me.
Keep pushing and the only thing you will get is asked to leave.
COMMAND: You have no authority over me. This conversation is over.
I don't take orders from strangers. Walk away.
Who do you think you are? I answer to no one.
I take orders from the council. Last I checked, you are not on it.
You speak like someone used to being obeyed. I am not used to obeying.
Ordering me around is a good way to get nothing at all.
I do not recall enlisting under your command.
That works on soldiers, maybe. I am a shopkeeper.
You can demand all you want. I am not obligated to comply.
I heard you. I am choosing not to answer.
Save the authority for someone who is impressed by it.
I take instructions from one person, and they are not in this room.
You do not have the rank to order me around. Or any rank, as far as I can tell.
Barking orders at civilians is not how this town works.
I answer to the people who pay me, not to strangers passing through.
The way you say 'tell me' suggests I have no choice. I assure you, I do.
You can phrase it as a command all you want. It is still a request, and the answer is no.
I do not know what authority you think you carry, but it does not extend to me.
I am under no obligation to tell you anything.
If you want cooperation, try asking. Commands get you nothing here.
You sound very official. That does not change anything.
Strangers do not get to walk in and start giving orders. That is not how it works.
I am sure that tone opens doors for you elsewhere. Not here.
Commanding me will not make me remember things I do not know.
I respect authority when it is earned. You have not earned mine.
You carry yourself like a captain, but I did not sign up for your crew.
I am going to pretend you asked politely and still say no.
If I answered every person who barked at me, I would never stop talking.
That directive might carry weight somewhere else. Here it means nothing.
I do not owe you answers, regardless of how firmly you ask.
PERSUASION: Save the heartfelt speeches. I've said all I'm going to say.
Nice try, but I'm not that easy to sway.
I can see what you're doing. The answer is no.
I appreciate the appeal, but my mind is made up.
You make a good case, but I cannot help you with this.
I hear you. I understand. The answer is still no.
You are very convincing, but not convincing enough.
Talking about the common good is nice. It does not change my situation.
I sympathize, truly. But I am not in a position to share more.
You have a way with words. Shame it will not work on me today.
I wish I could help. I really do. But this is not something I can talk about.
Your concern is genuine. I can tell. But I still cannot say more.
I admire the sentiment, but sentiments do not protect me if things go wrong.
You almost had me there. Almost.
I like you, I really do. But liking you does not mean I can trust you.
If it were simple, I would tell you. It is not simple.
Your argument is sound, but the risk is mine, not yours.
I want to believe you mean well. That does not mean I should talk.
You appeal to my better nature, but my better nature has gotten me in trouble before.
Helping you would mean hurting someone else. I cannot do that.
That was well said. But well said and wise are not the same thing.
Maybe you are right. But right does not always mean safe.
You speak from the heart. I speak from experience. And experience says stay quiet.
I have heard worse pleas and better ones. Neither changed my answer.
Your intentions are good. My silence is also good, for different reasons.
I cannot tell you what you want to hear. I am sorry.
If I thought talking would actually fix things, I would. But it will not.
I understand your argument. I simply disagree with the conclusion.
You have a gift for words. But words are cheap, and what you are asking is not.
I hear the sincerity. I do. But I have learned the hard way to keep my mouth shut.
DECEPTION: I don't believe you for a second. Nice try, though.
That doesn't add up, and we both know it. Stop wasting my time.
You are a terrible liar. We are done here.
That story has more holes in it than a fishing net.
I was not born yesterday. Try that on someone else.
You almost had me. Almost. But the details gave you away.
I have heard better lies from my five-year-old nephew.
Something about that does not sit right. I think I will pass.
You sound very confident for someone making things up.
If that were true, you would not need to try so hard to convince me.
I know that trick. You are fishing, and I am not biting.
An interesting claim. Unfortunately, it is obviously false.
Nice story. I do not believe a word of it.
You are trying to lead me somewhere. I can feel it.
I have dealt with enough liars to know one when I see one.
That is a bold claim for someone who cannot look me in the eye.
I do not know what you are playing at, but I am not playing along.
You seem to think I am gullible. I am not.
The more you embellish, the less I believe.
Save the performance. I am not your audience.
If you have to explain why it is true, it probably is not.
I spoke to the person you mentioned. They told a very different story.
I know a setup when I hear one. This conversation is over.
That contradicts something you said two minutes ago.
The vagueness tells me everything I need to know.
I cannot tell if you believe your own lies or if you are just brazen.
You are clever, I will give you that. But not clever enough.
That sounded rehearsed. Real stories do not come out that polished.
I have nothing to gain by playing along with whatever this is.
My trust is earned, not tricked. You have failed at both.
CHARM: Flattery will get you nowhere with me.
Save your charm for someone who cares. I'm not interested.
You think a few kind words will make me talk? Think again.
That is sweet, but sweetness does not buy information.
I am flattered. Still not talking, though.
You have a nice smile. It will not change my answer.
I can tell you are trying to be likable. It is not going to work.
You are pleasant enough, but pleasant does not mean trustworthy.
I have been complimented by experts. You are not there yet.
That warmth of yours: is it real, or just a tool?
Charm is nice in a social setting. This is not a social setting.
You remind me of someone who also talked their way into things. It did not end well.
If I gave up secrets every time someone was nice to me, I would have none left.
I appreciate the kindness. I really do. But no.
You are trying to create a bond. I see it. And I am opting out.
I like good conversation as much as the next person, but that is all this will be.
Friendly strangers make me more cautious, not less.
That was a lovely compliment. My answer remains the same.
You are very personable. I am very private. Impasse.
I have a weakness for good manners. But not that much of a weakness.
I do not open up to people just because they are agreeable.
Being charming is not the same as being trustworthy.
I can see you are making an effort, and I appreciate it. But no.
If charm were currency, you would be rich. But it is not, so you are out of luck.
That was well played. I am still not saying anything.
You make a good first impression. Let us leave it at that.
I am not the type to be won over by a warm voice and a steady gaze.
You are endearing. Truly. But endearing people still do not get to hear my secrets.
I like you. That does not mean I trust you.
You are easy to talk to. So I will make this easy: I have nothing more to say.
SLEIGHT: They catch your hand before you reach the item. The conversation is over.
You fumble and they notice immediately. Trust is gone.
Your fingers brush the edge, but they pull it away just in time.
They shift their weight and the item disappears into a pocket before you can reach it.
Your hand moves but they are watching too closely. No opportunity.
The angle is wrong. You cannot reach it without them seeing.
You knock something off the table in the attempt. They glare at you.
Your sleeve catches on a nail and the motion becomes too obvious.
They turn back at the worst possible moment. You pretend to scratch your arm.
The pouch is tied with a knot you cannot work without looking down.
Your hand closes on empty air. They already moved the item.
They are standing too close to the table. There is no clean reach.
A bystander glances your way and you abort the attempt.
The object is heavier than expected. You cannot lift it one-handed without noise.
Your palms are sweating. You cannot get a grip.
They notice your hand hovering and step back. The window closes.
You reach for it but your timing is off. They look down and you withdraw.
The item is pinned under their elbow. Moving it would require shoving them.
Your finger catches the edge but the item skitters across the table. They look.
You wait for the right moment, but it never comes. They are too attentive.
The clasp is more secure than it looked. You cannot unfasten it subtly.
They pat their pocket absently, right as you were about to go for it.
Your distraction fails. They keep their eyes on the table the whole time.
The item is in an interior pocket. There is no reaching it without contact.
You try to slide the paper away, but it makes a sound against the wood.
They place their hand over the item mid-conversation, as if by habit.
Your fingers are numb from the cold. Fine motor control is not there.
You make your move but a creak in the floorboard gives you away.
The letter is folded into their vest. It would take surgery to extract it unnoticed.
They notice your lingering handshake and pull away with a suspicious look.
REFLEX: You react too slowly. The moment passes and there is nothing to show for it.
Whatever it was, it's gone now. You missed your chance.
By the time you move, it is already too late.
Your hand shoots out but closes on nothing. Too slow.
The object hits the ground before you can react. It shatters.
You flinch in the right direction but not fast enough.
They moved and you hesitated. The window is gone.
Something fell off the shelf. You stared at it the whole way down.
By the time you turned your head, whatever flew past the window was gone.
You reached for it, but your reflexes betrayed you. It slipped through your fingers.
The sound came and went before you could locate it. Just an echo now.
They tossed the scrap into the fire. You watched it curl and burn.
The cup rolls off the table and crashes. You were a half-second behind it.
A flash of movement in the corner of your eye, but when you turn, nothing.
You lunge, but your foot catches and you stumble. The moment is lost.
They snatched it off the table faster than you could blink. It is gone.
The bird was already out of sight by the time you looked up.
You tried to grab the paper but the wind took it first.
Your body moved but your brain was a step behind. Nothing to show for it.
The shadow passed through your peripheral vision and vanished before you could focus.
They pulled their hand away and you grasped at air.
The door slammed shut before you could stick your foot in.
You blinked at the wrong second. Whatever happened, you missed it.
Your instincts told you to move. Your body disagreed.
It clattered to the floor and rolled under the cabinet. You just watched.
They flung it over their shoulder. You dove and came up empty.
The trapdoor shut with a bang. You reached it just a moment too late.
You saw it coming but could not make your legs cooperate in time.
A gust scattered the loose pages before you could pin them down.
The insect disappeared into the crack in the wall. You were not even close.
ENDURANCE: You gag on the first sip. They smirk and close up.
You can't take it. They shake their head, clearly unimpressed.
Your composure breaks and they lose all interest in talking.
The smell alone makes your eyes water. You cannot get close enough to investigate.
You last about three minutes in the heat before you have to step out.
Your stomach turns and you excuse yourself. When you come back, they are distant.
The brew hits your throat like fire. You cough and they take the mug back.
You try to keep a straight face, but the taste defeats you. They laugh.
The wait stretches on and your patience gives out first. They notice.
You can feel the cold biting through your clothes. You start shivering and they lose interest.
The dust in the cellar sets you coughing. You cannot stay down there long enough.
You try to eat what they offer, but your body rejects it. Conversation over.
The stench is overwhelming. You cover your nose and they take offense.
Your legs give out from standing so long. They do not seem impressed.
The smoke stings your eyes until you can barely see. You retreat.
You cannot hold the position they asked you to. They shrug and walk away.
The cold water takes your breath away. You gasp and they look disappointed.
Halfway through the task, your hands start shaking and you have to stop.
The sun beats down and you wilt visibly. They decide you are not worth the trouble.
You try to keep up but the pace is too much. They leave you behind.
Your endurance fails you and they see it. Whatever trust was building evaporates.
The bitter root makes you wince. They take that as your answer.
You shift your weight too often. They read it as weakness and clam up.
The foul air in the tunnel forces you back before you reach the end.
You try to sit through the ceremony, but it drags on and you fidget. They notice.
The local spirit burns all the way down. You sputter and they pull it away.
The rain soaks through and you start complaining. They stop talking.
You wince at the cold and they lose confidence in you.
Your body gives out before your will does. They seem unsurprised.
The meal is punishment disguised as hospitality. You fail to disguise your disgust.
RESISTANCE: The pull is too strong. You find yourself nodding along and forgetting why you came.
A fog settles over your thoughts. By the time it clears, the conversation has moved on.
You feel a sudden urge to leave, and before you know it, you are already walking away.
A heaviness settles behind your eyes. You lose your train of thought entirely.
You cannot remember what you were about to ask. The words dissolve before they form.
Their voice seems to echo strangely, and for a moment you forget where you are.
A wave of fatigue washes over you. Your eyelids droop and the conversation slips away.
You feel a compulsion to agree with everything they say. It takes effort to even think clearly.
The room seems to tilt. By the time you steady yourself, the subject has changed.
Their words burrow into your thoughts and rearrange your priorities. You leave satisfied, though you cannot say why.
A chill runs through you and your resolve falters. You excuse yourself without knowing why.
Something about the air in the room makes it hard to concentrate.
You open your mouth to press the question, but the thought crumbles.
A warmth spreads through your chest and your urgency fades. Everything seems fine.
Their eyes catch yours and for a moment your mind goes perfectly blank.
You find yourself talking about something else entirely. How did you get here?
The pendant flashes and you lose a few seconds. When you blink, they are smiling.
A ringing in your ears drowns out the important thoughts. Only the trivial ones remain.
You were about to ask something important. Now you cannot remember what it was.
Your certainty evaporates. Maybe there really is nothing to worry about.
The pressure behind your eyes builds and you wince. They use the distraction to redirect.
Everything they say sounds perfectly reasonable, and you cannot find the flaw.
A prickling sensation spreads across your scalp. You feel watched from the inside.
The conviction that brought you here drains away like water through sand.
You want to push back, but something whispers that it would be rude.
Their words coat your thoughts like honey. Sweet, heavy, and impossible to see through.
For a moment you feel like a guest who has overstayed. You apologize and start to leave.
A pleasant numbness settles over your mind. Questions seem unnecessary.
You feel as though the answer you got was perfectly satisfactory, even though it told you nothing.
The thought slips away like a fish. You grasp at it, but it is already gone.
